Block deletion of paid students and observers from registrations

  creditAppliedSet, and Stripe-paid status against the pending set

  badges when the attendee has been paid for (cash, Stripe, or credit)

  returns 400 "This dancer has already been paid for and cannot be removed."

commit.message:
Support large files (2 GB) and video formats (MP4, MKV, AVI, MOV, WMV)

Backend:
- Chunked file writing (8 MB chunks) to handle large uploads without OOM
- 2 GB max upload size with proper 413 error
- Video support: MP4, MKV, AVI, MOV, WMV, FLV, TS, WEBM
- Audio extraction from video via PyAV (fallback to direct Whisper decode)
- Cleanup extracted audio files after transcription
- Sanitized filenames on upload

Frontend:
- File input accepts audio/* and video/* formats
- Upload zone shows supported audio + video formats and 2 GB limit

About page updated with v2.1 features (video support, large files)

commit.message:
Sessions 3-5: Transversal Zero-Sum framework, 2-adic cascade, Syracuse duality

Session 3: Discovered Transversal Zero-Sum Barrier (direction 0D).
  U = Z/DZ always, but 0 not in R (restricted sumset). Theorems T1-T5.

Session 4: 2-adic cascade method (C1-C6). WLOG i1=0, n=1 classification,
  k<=4 no-cycles, verified p<=46 including near-critical (46,29).

Session 5: Syracuse-Cascade Duality (C7-C10). Cascade remainders track
  Syracuse orbit exactly. k<=30 no-cycles proved. Extended to p<=60
  (83 pairs, 3.3M candidates). Confirmed equivalence barrier: cascade
  IS the Collatz iteration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(inbound): remove nós orphan/auto-reset, desativa trilha GLM

Removidos (5 nós):
- Supabase - Pausa Conversa (duplicado solto, id: pausa-conversa)
- Trilha auto-reset: Supabase - Auto Reset + Evolution - Envia Reset Sim
  + Wait 5s + HTTP - Dispara Outbound (já estava desconectada)

Desativados/desconectados (8 nós — trilha GLM):
- Conversa Encerrada? → Supabase - Histórico Completo → Code - Monta Prompt GLM
  → HTTP - GLM Avalia Conversa → Code - Processa Resposta GLM
  → Supabase - Salva Descoberta → Auto-tune? → HTTP - Dispara Auto-tune
- Desconectado de Resposta Ainda Válida? para religar depois

commit.message:
fix: 13 playtest-critical bugs — crash guards, race conditions, commodity fixes

- simDay: chemicals contract no longer deducts from syntheticRubber (wrong resource)
- simDay: supply_chain perk check moved outside per-tire loop (perf)
- simDay: training boost no longer compounds across locations
- simDay: auto-restock budget raised to 25% of cash, cap to $2M
- exchangeTick: portfolio undefined guard on buyer/seller fill processing
- exchangeTick: sentiment undefined guard (init if missing)
- exchangeTick: orderBook bids/asks null guard for AI trade intents
- exchange: reset dailyVolume each tick (was accumulating forever, killing price impact)
- profile: send-cash wrapped in withPlayerLock (race condition fix)
- wholesale: block self-buy
- factory: remove hard rubber requirement for players without rubber infrastructure
- commodityMarket: fix chemicals sell-to-world deducting from syntheticRubber
- BottomNav: guard g.storage, g.loans, g.unlockedSuppliers for new players
- FranchisePanel: add city names to location selector, fix confirm() for Capacitor
- SupplierPanel: add $500K/$1M/$2M auto-restock budget options

commit.message:
revert: restore original block→world formula (WorldXMin/WorldZMax as origin)

Previous "fix" based on comparing territory centroid to heart position was
incorrect — the heart can be anywhere within the territory, not at the
centroid. Reverting to the original formula which distributes free plots
correctly across the map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AttributeError: keep _current_tile_buffer init for live frame handler safety

_on_tile_zstack_frame still fires during tile workflows (camera sends
frames regardless). It references _current_tile_buffer which was removed
from prepare_for_tile_workflows. Restore the init to None so the handler
doesn't crash — it will buffer frames harmlessly but the disk path
handles the actual 3D volume building.

fix: resolve TypeScript null type errors in task-list and new project page

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker permissions: Create logs directory with proper ownership

Backend was failing to start with EACCES error when Winston logger
tried to create /app/logs directory. The container switches to 'node'
user before the logger initialization, so the logs directory must be
created with proper ownership in the Dockerfile.

Changes:
- Updated RUN command to create both /app/uploads and /app/logs
- Set ownership for both directories to node:node user
- This allows Winston logger to write log files after USER node switch

Fixes: Error: EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/app/logs'

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove clock defaults — unreliable on Tahoe, set via System Settings instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(db): replace SQLite with PGlite (PostgreSQL WASM) (#2598)

* docs: add infrastructure architecture design

Proposes unified architecture to eliminate infrastructure divergence between local and cloud deployments:
- Single SQL dialect (PostgreSQL everywhere via PGlite locally)
- Unified monitoring pipeline (OTel → Parquet → DuckDB)
- Zero external dependencies for local development
- Maintains cloud-native scaling with Kubernetes, NATS, and OTel Collector

Addresses monitoring data bloat, SQLite/PostgreSQL divergence, and local setup complexity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): add @electric-sql/pglite dependency (smoke test passed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): add kysely-pglite dialect, remove kysely-bun-worker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): replace SQLite factory with PGlite (PostgreSQL WASM)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(monitoring): remove SQLite dialect branching, use PostgreSQL everywhere

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(event-bus): remove SQLite RETURNING fallback, PGlite supports it natively

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(migrate): use information_schema everywhere, drop sqlite_master

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: update DatabaseType to pglite|postgres across context factory and event bus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove all remaining sqlite references and BunWorkerDialect imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): seed FK parent records for PGlite compatibility

PGlite (PostgreSQL) enforces FK constraints unlike SQLite in test mode.
Add seedCommonTestFixtures helper and update all affected test files to
create parent organization/user records before inserting FK-dependent data.
Also update .gitignore for PGlite data directories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(migrations): remove dead SQLite branches from migrations 024, 026, 031

With PGlite, the PostgreSQL path always runs. Remove isPostgres runtime
detection and SQLite fallback code (table recreation for DROP COLUMN, etc).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove databaseType from MeshContextConfig (no longer needed)

No consumer of MeshContextConfig uses databaseType anymore — monitoring
storage, event bus, and migration runner all resolve dialect internally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(db): migrate integer boolean columns to native BOOLEAN type

Convert is_error (monitoring_logs) and enabled (event_subscriptions) from
integer 0/1 to native PostgreSQL BOOLEAN. Update all code to use boolean
values directly instead of integer comparisons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): fix remaining enabled: number → boolean in event-bus inserts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): fix PGlite singleton sharing and add plugin migrations to test schema

- getDbDialect() now reuses the singleton PGlite instance via getDb()
  to prevent file lock conflicts when Better Auth and the app both need
  a database connection
- createTestSchema() now runs plugin migrations so plugin startup hooks
  (e.g. workflow recovery) don't fail on missing tables
- Update e2e workflow step name to remove outdated SQLite reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): default PGlite path to ~/deco/db.pglite for stable location

Use a fixed path under the user's home directory so the database is
consistent regardless of the working directory the server is started from.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(db): remove all remaining SQLite references across codebase

- Replace sqlite_master queries with information_schema in CLI export
- Migrate plugin test helpers from BunWorkerDialect to PGlite
- Update outdated comments mentioning SQLite to reference PGlite
- Update AGENTS.md documentation to reflect PGlite as default

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(db): remove sqlite:// backward compat and update deploy docs

- Remove deprecated sqlite:// URL handler from database factory
- Remove corresponding backward compat test
- Update deploy README and env.example to reference PGlite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): increase timeout for multi-org stress test with PGlite

PGlite is slower than SQLite for heavy concurrent workloads; increase
the timeout from 5s (default) to 15s to avoid flaky CI failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address cubic review findings — singleton invariant, SQL injection, test coverage

- Replace unreachable PGlite fallback in getDbDialect with explicit error throw
- Escape single quotes in table names before SQL interpolation in export command
- Add missing .isError checks for columns/constraints/indexes queries
- Fix test to actually verify default (no URL) database creation path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): restore test isolation for PGlite default path test

Use explicit file: URL with tempDir instead of no-arg createDatabase()
which writes to ~/deco/db.pglite and leaves persistent artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(docs): remove infrastructure architecture design plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): scope constraint query join by table OID to prevent cross-joins

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): use getDatabaseUrl() as fallback in parseDatabaseUrl

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): externalize kysely-codegen to fix build:server bundling failure

kysely-pglite pulls in kysely-codegen as a runtime dependency, which has
dynamic require() calls for optional DB dialect packages (tarn, tedious,
mysql2, better-sqlite3, etc.) that aren't installed. Add kysely-codegen to
an ALWAYS_EXTERNAL list so the bundler skips resolving its optional imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add build:server smoke test to CI workflow

Catches bundling failures (missing externals, unresolvable imports) on PRs
before merge. Verifies server.js, migrate.js, and cli.js are produced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): address PR review findings — singleton lifecycle, cleanup, stale refs

- Fix PGlite singleton lifecycle: null out dbInstance in closeDatabase()
  when the closed instance matches the singleton, preventing stale refs
- Add runtime warning in getDbDialect() when PGlite URL mismatches singleton
- Harden ensurePGliteDirectory() to throw in production instead of silent
  in-memory fallback
- Remove vestigial _dbType parameter from migrate.ts function stack
- Fix stale FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED JSDoc in event-bus storage
- Extract mapRowToSubscription() helper replacing 4 duplicate mappings
- Fix CLI help text default DATABASE_URL to ~/deco/db.pglite
- Fix benchmark path from .db to .pglite with updated comment
- Remove dead "UNIQUE constraint" SQLite error branch in create-session
- Update stale SQLite references to PGlite in comments
- Add PGlite instance cleanup in plugin test files with try/catch guards

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): address cubic review round 2 — cleanup, help text, error cause

- Fix benchmark teardown: use rmSync with recursive:true for PGlite dirs
- Fix CLI help text: use $HOME instead of ~ for DATABASE_URL default
- Chain original error with { cause: error } in production fallback throw

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): increase timeout for OAuth proxy E2E tests hitting external servers

Apify MCP server responses were exceeding the default 5s Bun test timeout,
causing flaky CI failures. Set 15s timeout for all tests in this file since
they make real HTTP requests to external MCP servers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review — constraint index filtering and PGlite close handling

- Only add PRIMARY KEY and UNIQUE constraints to constraintIndexNames set,
  preventing valid user-created indexes from being incorrectly filtered out
- Narrow PGlite close error handling in test teardown to only ignore the
  expected "is closed" error, rethrowing unexpected failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): narrow PGlite close error handling to only suppress expected errors

Bare catch blocks were swallowing all pglite.close() errors. Now only
the expected "PGlite is closed" error is suppressed; unexpected errors
(IO failures, corruption) are rethrown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(backtest): StrategyRouter — exclusive regime routing + empty initial state

Strategies were activating simultaneously (additive) instead of exclusively.
In BEAR_TREND: DCA + trend_follower + SMC all ran together → inflated trade counts
and identical Sharpe across all strategies.

Changes:
- _compute_target_strategies: exclusive priority-based routing
  (bull_trend → TF only, bear_trend → DCA only, breakout/volatile → SMC only)
- Initial _active_strategies: set() instead of all-4 to prevent pre-regime
  bar inflation (was causing identical Sharpe -23.857 for DCA/TF/SMC)
- reset(): same fix for clean backtest re-runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add product lines: backend catalog grouping for channel gating

Product lines are operational categories (e.g., "Trading Card Supplies")
that control which products are visible/syncable to which channels.
Distinct from Shopify collections (customer-facing merchandising).

- Schema: product_lines, product_line_products, channel_product_lines tables
- Migration seeds default line + assigns all existing products/channels
- CRUD API: create/edit lines, assign products, assign channels
- Feed discovery and sync engine gated by product line assignments
- Feed enable endpoint validates product line before creating feed
- Allocation grid supports productLineId filter param
- New Product Lines management page with expandable detail rows
- Allocation page shows product line dropdown filter (when >1 line exists)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: V8 gap closure — FEC recipe, UI bins, trend axes, eye enum, data_rate auto-detect

7-phase implementation closing all software-addressable V8 assessment gaps:

Phase 1: FEC recipe + workflow + report integration
- New fec-check recipe (Tier 2 SI): verify FEC enabled, error rate, frame
  count, optional bin distribution, configurable soak duration
- FECCheckConfig frozen model with lane/rate/frame/bin validation
- 3 workflow templates updated with fec-check steps
- Diagnostic HTML report gains FEC counters section
- DiagnosticSummary gains fec_status field

Phase 2: FEC UI error bins toggle
- Show Error Bins switch adds 7 bin columns (Bin0-Bin6) to counter table

Phase 3: Register diff category selector
- Multi-select dropdown for global/per_link/tx_eq/error_stats/serdes
- Both baseline and compare operations use selected categories

Phase 4: Trend chart dual Y-axis separation
- Temperature on y1 (left), voltage on y2, link width on y3, errors on y4
- Prevents scale confusion between °C, mV, lane count, and error counts

Phase 5: BathtubRequest/HeatmapRequest eye type
- Changed eye field from str+regex to EyeSelect enum with string coercion
- Removed _parse_eye() and _EYE_MAP; Pydantic handles validation

Phase 6: Event bus cleanup on disconnect
- Added remove_event_bus(handle) called from _cleanup_device_state()

Phase 7: Recipe data_rate auto-detection
- data_rate defaults to None on PRBSSweep, EyeMarginSweep, LoopbackBER,
  LaneIsolate configs
- resolve_data_rate() reads live link speed, falls back to GEN6_64G
- Updated callers in tier2_signal.py and tier3_debug.py

1,682 tests passing, 73% coverage. Security and code review clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
